title: rubus phoenicacanthus
intro: Rubus phoenicacanthus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan raspberry rose, is a species of bramble native to the Himalayas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus phoenicacanthus include treating digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol and blood pressure. Herbal preparations of Rubus phoenicacanthus can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
